summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: FedNow advances over hurdles
articles.description: The Federal Reserve’s instant payments system is adding banks and accounts, but building more use cases to spur uptake remains a challenge.

articles.title: Why Cash App wants food orders
articles.description: Block aims to meld its Square merchants network with Cash App users to push local dining, boosting the app’s stickiness and payments volume.

articles.title: Fraud fight can’t wait on government
articles.description: “While we wait for federal roundtable policies to trickle down, merchants are suffering heavily right now,” writes one industry consultant.

articles.title: Merchants blast credit union rule
articles.description: Retail and restaurant groups say federal credit union regulators imposed an “unlawful” rule preempting an Illinois law that curbs payment card fees.

articles.title: Debit cards top payments, Fed says
articles.description: While the cards still dominate payments, growth in their use for 2024 was the slowest since a triennial Federal Reserve report started in 2001.

articles.title: Cash App triggers $45M settlement
articles.description: Block misled consumers about the safety of using the financial app and failed to help users who were fraud victims, 46 states alleged.

articles.title: U.S. Bank pursues payments-first strategy
articles.description: With payments as the entry point to banking relationships, the lender cultivates a low-cost funding base, said executive Arijit Roy.

articles.title: Fiserv president exits
articles.description: As Dhivya Suryadevara announced her resignation Tuesday, the company elevated two other executives.
